description_long: This object consists of two large machete-style knives, each featuring a long, straight, single-edged blade with a slightly rounded tip. The blades are made of dark, weathered metal, showing signs of wear and use, with a mottled, matte finish. Each knife has a distinct handle: one is black and the other is brown, both made of what appears to be wood or composite material, attached with visible rivets. The handles are straight with a slight ergonomic curve for grip, and lack any crossguard or elaborate decoration. The overall design is utilitarian, suggesting these knives are intended for heavy-duty cutting or clearing tasks. The size and construction indicate they are robust tools, suitable for outdoor or urban utility use.
description: A pair of large, weathered machete knives with long, straight blades and simple, riveted handles in black and brown. The design is utilitarian and robust, with no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Two machete knives are shown side by side, clearly displaying their full blade length and the contrasting black and brown handles.
description_view_1: The knives are viewed edge-on, emphasizing the thinness and straightness of the blades, with the brown handle more visible.
description_view_2: A low, angled view highlights the flatness of the blades and the alignment of the handles, showing minimal blade thickness.
description_view_3: The knives are shown from the opposite side, with both blades and handles fully visible, emphasizing the utilitarian design.
attribute_handle_design: straight, slightly contoured, riveted, black and brown wood or composite
